description: FARC dissidents are former members of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) who refused to demobilize after the 2016 peace process. They are involved in drug trafficking and armed conflict with Colombian forces and rival groups. Operating primarily in Colombia and Venezuela, they consist of multiple factions such as the Central General Staff and Second Marquetalia. The group is led by former mid-level commanders and has an estimated 2,000-2,500 armed combatants.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 15 July 2018, the Colombian and Peruvian governments launched a joint military effort known as Operation Armageddon to combat FARC dissidents. Peru issued a 60-day state of emergency in its Putumayo province...
country_links.context: FARC dissidents were the target of a joint Colombian-Peruvian military operation in Peru's Putumayo province.

country_links.evidence_quote: Jhon 40 and their dissident 43rd Front moved into the Amazonas state of western Venezuela where they can operate with Colombian allies. Venezuela has served as the primary location for many FARC dissidents.
country_links.context: FARC dissidents have moved into and operate from Venezuela, which serves as their primary location.
